hook: ChatGPT gave me a 9-step plan when I asked for help with one email.
caption:
ChatGPT gave me a 9-step plan when I asked for help with one email.

I didn't ask for a framework. I didn't ask for headers. I definitely didn't ask it to start with 'Great question! Here's a comprehensive breakdown...'

I asked it to help me sound less passive-aggressive to my landlord.

The problem isn't the AI. The problem is it doesn't know that you're a 'just tell me the answer' person — not a 'walk me through the methodology' person.

Some people want the full reasoning. Some people want the punchline. Some people need bullet points or their brain checks out. Some people hate bullet points because it feels like a PowerPoint deck threw up on their screen.

AI defaults to one mode: thorough, structured, and slightly exhausting.

Your BootFile tells it which mode is actually yours.

So instead of getting the 9-step email framework... you get the two sentences that fix the email.

That's the difference between AI that's impressive and AI that's actually useful.
